skill.name: amazon-best-selling-products-finder-api-skill
skill.description: Extract structured best-selling product data from Amazon via the BrowserAct API. Agent should apply this skill when users need Amazon product data, competitor pricing, sales volume, or market research across Amazon marketplaces.
skill.summary: Automated Amazon product data extraction via BrowserAct API, returning titles, prices, ratings, reviews, and sales volume.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: After evaluating 63 static analysis findings across 2 files (199 lines), the confirmed risks are: network access to BrowserAct API (legitimate, the skill purpose is API-based data extraction) and environment variable access for BROWSERACT_API_KEY (required for authentication). 29 external_commands findings in SKILL.md are FALSE POSITIVES - the scanner misidentified Markdown code block delimiters and inline backticks as Ruby/shell backtick execution (SKILL.md is documentation, not a shell script). 6 weak cryptographic algorithm findings in SKILL.md are FALSE POSITIVES - the scanner flagged Markdown horizontal rules (---) and heading markers (##). 2 system reconnaissance findings are FALSE POSITIVES - they are documentation text. No malicious intent, obfuscation, data exfiltration, or prompt injection detected. Risk level is MEDIUM because the skill makes external API calls and accesses credentials, which is expected and documented behavior for an API-based data extraction tool.

medium_findings.title: Network access to external API
medium_findings.description: The Python script makes HTTP requests to api.browseract.com to run workflow tasks, poll status, and retrieve results. This is expected and required for the skill to function as a BrowserAct API client. The API endpoint is hardcoded and not user-configurable.

medium_findings.title: Environment variable credential access
medium_findings.description: The script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from environment variables for authenticating with the BrowserAct API. This is standard credential handling for an API client. The API key is sent only to the intended BrowserAct API endpoint.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in documentation
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains hardcoded URLs to BrowserAct console and Amazon marketplace domains. These are instructional URLs in documentation text, not executable code. No risk of unauthorized network requests.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown formatting misidentified as shell execution
dangerous_patterns.description: 29 static findings flagged SKILL.md backtick characters as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. However, SKILL.md is a Markdown documentation file. All flagged backticks are Markdown code block delimiters (```bash ... ```) or inline code formatting (`...`), not shell commands. These are all FALSE POSITIVES.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown formatting misidentified as weak cryptography
dangerous_patterns.description: 6 static findings flagged SKILL.md lines with 'weak cryptographic algorithm'. The flagged characters are Markdown horizontal rules (---) in YAML frontmatter and heading markers (##), not cryptographic operations. These are all FALSE POSITIVES.

content.user_title: Extract Amazon Best Selling Products with API
content.value_statement: Extracting product data from Amazon manually is slow and error-prone. This skill automates the process by using the BrowserAct API to return structured data including product titles, prices, ratings, reviews, sales volume, and promotional details.
actual_capabilities: Extract Amazon product titles, prices, ratings, and review counts in a structured format
Search for best selling products across different Amazon marketplaces by region
Retrieve sales volume data, stock availability, and promotional badge details
Monitor competitor pricing and product listings with configurable result limits
Run cross-region comparisons across Amazon.com, Amazon.co.uk, and Amazon.de marketplaces
Collect product ASINs, categories, and direct product URLs for further analysis
limitations: Requires a valid BrowserAct API key set as the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable
Task execution may take several minutes due to automated browser operations by the API
Limited to the data fields and structure provided by the BrowserAct workflow template
Cannot extract data if the BrowserAct service has its own rate limits or restrictions

use_cases.title: Market Trend Analysis
use_cases.description: Extract product listings, ratings, and sales volumes for specific keywords to identify market trends and demand patterns in any product category.

use_cases.title: Competitor Price Monitoring
use_cases.description: Track competitor pricing, discounts, and promotions over time to optimize your own pricing strategy and stay competitive in the marketplace.

use_cases.title: Cross-Region Product Comparison
use_cases.description: Compare product availability, pricing, and ratings across different Amazon marketplaces to identify arbitrage opportunities and regional trends.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Product Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the best selling [product category] on Amazon and return the top [number] products with their titles, prices, and ratings.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick product discovery for initial market research

prompt_templates.title: Competitor Pricing Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Amazon for [product name] and extract the current prices, list prices, and sales volumes. Compare this data across different sellers.

prompt_templates.title: Cross-Region Market Comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for [product name] on Amazon.com, Amazon.co.uk, and Amazon.de. Compare the prices, availability, and ratings across all three regions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Identifying regional price differences and availability

prompt_templates.title: Trending Products Discovery
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the top 20 best selling products in the [category] category on Amazon. For each product return the title, current price, star rating, review count, past month sales volume, and any promotion badges.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive product trend analysis with full data fields

output_examples.input: Find the top 5 best selling gaming mice on Amazon and show their prices, ratings, and review counts.
output_examples.output:
Product: Logitech G502 Hero - $49.99 | Rating: 4.6/5 | Reviews: 32,450
Product: Razer DeathAdder V2 - $44.99 | Rating: 4.5/5 | Reviews: 28,120
Product: SteelSeries Rival 600 - $59.99 | Rating: 4.4/5 | Reviews: 15,780
Product: Corsair Harpoon RGB - $29.99 | Rating: 4.5/5 | Reviews: 22,340
Product: Glorious Model O - $49.99 | Rating: 4.6/5 | Reviews: 18,900

output_examples.input: Compare iPhone 15 prices and availability across Amazon US and UK marketplaces.
output_examples.output:
Amazon US: iPhone 15 128GB - $799.00 | In Stock | 4.6 stars (8,200 reviews)
Amazon UK: iPhone 15 128GB - £799.00 | In Stock | 4.5 stars (3,450 reviews)

Price difference: UK price is approximately 15% higher after currency conversion.

output_examples.input: What are the trending kitchen gadgets this month on Amazon? Show sales volume and promotions.
output_examples.output:
1. Air Fryer Pro Max 8QT - $89.99 | 4.7 stars | 5,200 sold/month | Lightning Deal: 20% off
2. Instant Pot Duo Plus - $79.99 | 4.6 stars | 4,800 sold/month | No promotion
3. Ninja Creami Ice Cream Maker - $199.99 | 4.4 stars | 3,100 sold/month | Coupon: $25 off

best_practices: Set the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable before calling the script and never hardcode it in the script or share it with others.
Start with a small limit value like 5 for quick testing before running full extraction with higher limits.
Use specific, targeted keywords for more relevant product results and faster task completion.
anti_patterns: Do not commit your BrowserAct API key to version control or share it in logs, error messages, or documentation.
Do not set the limit to very high values without first testing with a smaller sample to verify the results are correct.
Do not ignore error messages that indicate an invalid or expired API key; check the BrowserAct console before retrying.

faq.question: What is BrowserAct API?
faq.answer: BrowserAct is a third-party service that provides automated browser workflow templates for data extraction, including Amazon product data.

faq.question: Do I need a BrowserAct account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a BrowserAct account and a valid API key set as the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: How long does product extraction take?
faq.answer: Extraction may take several minutes because the BrowserAct API runs automated browser operations to collect the product data.

faq.question: Can I search across different Amazon regions?
faq.answer: Yes, you can set the marketplace URL parameter to any Amazon domain such as amazon.co.uk or amazon.de for region-specific searches.

faq.question: What data fields are returned by this skill?
faq.answer: The result includes title, brand, list price, current price, star rating, review count, sales volume, availability, promotion, ASIN, category, badge, and product URL.

faq.question: What happens if the extraction fails?
faq.answer: The script automatically retries once. If it fails again, check your API key validity and network connection before reporting the error.
